full_response:
We are given the curve in ℝ³  
 v(λ) = ( x(λ), y(λ), z(λ) ) = ( sin(λ²),  ln(λ+1),  2λ² − 1 )  
and asked to compute the dot‐product  
 v(λ)·v(λ) = |v(λ)|² = x(λ)² + y(λ)² + z(λ)².  

Step 1.  Identify the components:  
 x(λ) = sin(λ²)  
 y(λ) = ln(λ+1)  
 z(λ) = 2λ² − 1  

Step 2.  Square each component:  
 x(λ)² = [sin(λ²)]² = sin²(λ²)  
 y(λ)² = [ln(λ+1)]² = (ln(λ+1))²  
 z(λ)² = (2λ² − 1)² = 4λ⁴ − 4λ² + 1  

Step 3.  Sum them:  
 v(λ)·v(λ) = sin²(λ²)  +  (ln(λ+1))²  +  (2λ² − 1)²  
      = sin²(λ²)  +  (ln(λ+1))²  +  4λ⁴  −  4λ²  +  1  

Therefore the final result is  

\boxed{v(λ)\cdot v(λ)=\sin^{2}(\lambda^{2})+\bigl[\ln(λ+1)\bigr]^{2}+(2λ^{2}-1)^{2}.}  

Confidence: 100%
